name: Iran
summary: Primary target of US-Israeli strikes since Feb 28. Rejected US 15-point ceasefire plan March 25 as 'maximalist.' Issued 5-point counterproposal demanding sovereignty over Hormuz and reparations. IRGC Navy chief Tangsiri killed by Israel at Bandar Abbas March 26. IRGC formalized yuan-denominated toll booth at Hormuz. FM Araghchi: 'We do not want a ceasefire.' Denies all negotiations while Pakistan confirms message relay.

signals.content: US (Operation Epic Fury) and Israel (Operation Lion's Roar) launched coordinated surprise airstrikes on multiple sites across Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, Chief of Staff Mousavi, and former president Ahmadinejad. Weapons: Tomahawks, B-2/B-1/B-52 bombers, HIMARS. 201 killed, 747 injured on Day 1 across 24 provinces.

Iranian state media confirmed Khamenei's death. Iran responded with 500+ ballistic/naval missiles and ~2,000 drones targeting Israel, US bases, and Gulf states hosting US assets (UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Jordan).

IRGC announced Strait of Hormuz closed to US, Israeli, and Western allied ships. Missile launch rates declining from Day 1, analysts point to depletion of missile/launcher stores and rationing strategy for longer war.
Mojtaba Khamenei (age 56, son of Ali Khamenei) appointed new Supreme Leader by Assembly of Experts. His mother, wife, and sister were killed in the Feb 28 strike. He was not present at the compound.
Iranian government reported 1,255 killed in US-Israeli attacks, described as 'mostly civilians.' Independent group Hengaw later reported 5,300 killed in first 18 days (511 civilians, 4,789 military).

Mojtaba Khamenei issued first statement (read by state TV anchor, no video/audio of him released). Called for national unity, Hormuz to remain closed, demanded all US bases in region be closed or attacked.

Iran's FM Araghchi rejected ceasefire: 'The United States started it and is responsible for all consequences.' President Pezeshkian set conditions: US/Israel must recognize Iran's rights, pay reparations, guarantee no future war.

Israel struck Iran's South Pars gas field (world's largest, 70% of Iran's gas production) and Asaluyeh refinery. Damage affected ~12% of total gas output. Iran retaliated by striking Qatar's Ras Laffan, UAE gas fields, Saudi refinery, Kuwaiti gas units.

Iran categorically denied Trump's claim of 'productive conversations.' Foreign Ministry stated no negotiations, direct or indirect, are taking place. Fars and Tasnim (both IRGC-linked) confirmed zero contact with Washington. Mehr News Agency described Trump's remarks as attempt to 'influence energy markets and gain time.' An informed Iranian source claimed Trump's pause reflects pressure, not progress.

Iran fired new missile barrage at Israel including Tel Aviv, causing major building damage and at least 4 casualties. Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf called Trump's talk claims 'fakenews used to manipulate financial and oil markets.' IRGC issued sharp rebuke calling Trump a 'deceitful American president.' However, CBS News exclusively reported a senior Iranian FM official said 'we received points from the US through mediators and they are being reviewed' — Iran publicly denies but privately engaging via intermediaries. FM Araghchi stated Hormuz is 'open to ships from nations not backing US-Israeli aggression' — a selective blockade shift designed to split the coalition. Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr appointed new SNSC secretary replacing assassinated Ali Larijani. Tasnim published Iran's 'six conditions to end the war,' seeing 'no immediate prospect for ceasefire.' Police arresting citizens 'spreading propaganda in favor of the enemy'; internet blackout in 25th day.

Iran rejected US 15-point ceasefire plan, calling it 'extremely maximalist and unreasonable.' Issued 5-point counterproposal: end aggression, guarantee no recurrence, reparations, end all fronts, sovereignty over Hormuz. FM Araghchi: 'We do not want a ceasefire. We want the war to end on our terms.' (Sources: NPR, CNBC, Al Jazeera, PBS, The Hill)

IRGC Navy chief Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri killed in Israeli airstrike at Bandar Abbas at 3:00 AM. He was personally overseeing Hormuz closure. Confirmed by IDF, CENTCOM, Netanyahu. US Admiral told IRGC Navy to 'abandon your posts or die.' Iran has NOT confirmed death. Highest-ranking kill since Supreme Leader Khamenei on Feb 28. (Sources: IDF, CENTCOM, Al Jazeera, Haaretz, Jerusalem Post, Gulf News)

IRGC formalized yuan-denominated toll booth system at Hormuz. Ships submit details to IRGC intermediaries, receive code and escort through Iranian territorial waters around Larak Island. At least 2 vessels paid $2M each in yuan. 26 ships used system since March 13. 142 total transits March 1-25 vs 2,652 baseline = 95% reduction. (Sources: USNI News, Fortune, Foreign Policy, Al Jazeera, NBC, Lloyd's List Intelligence)

edges.context: Iran controls northern shore of Hormuz. IRGC Navy closed strait to US/Israel/Western allied ships since Mar 5. Now signaling selective opening for non-aggressor nations (India, China, Japan). Only 21 tankers transited since war began vs 100+/day pre-war.

Israel launched preemptive strikes on Iran Feb 28, struck nuclear sites (Natanz, Fordow, Isfahan), Kharg Island, South Pars. Iran retaliating with missiles on Israeli cities. New barrage hit Tel Aviv on Mar 24.

US launched Operation Epic Fury on Feb 28. 50,000 troops. Pentagon requesting $200B supplemental. Iran striking US bases. Back-channel via Turkey/Egypt/Pakistan intermediaries but Iran publicly denying all talks.
Hezbollah entered war on Mar 2 in response to Khamenei's killing. Iran's primary proxy in Lebanon.
Iran fired 314 ballistic missiles, 1,672 drones, 15 cruise missiles at UAE. Hit ADNOC Ruwais refinery, Dubai airport, US Consulate, Shah gas field, Fujairah port.
Iran struck Ras Laffan LNG facility in retaliation for South Pars attack. Destroyed LNG Trains 4 & 6. Qatar expelled Iranian attachés.
Iran's Hormuz closure + attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure directly caused oil to surge from ~$70 to $119/bbl peak. Selective blockade shift slightly eases but doesn't resolve.
Houthis are Iran's proxy in Yemen. Now announced entering war on Iran's side. Threatening Red Sea strikes. Would create second chokepoint.
Russia providing intelligence to Iran including data on US military positions.
China provided missile spare parts, advanced radar systems, BeiDou-3 encrypted navigation, satellite intelligence.

trigger_points.condition: Mojtaba Khamenei appears on video/audio (proof of life and command)
trigger_points.mechanism: New leader hasn't been seen. CIA/DIA testified Iran in 'deep command and control crisis.' If he appears, signals regime stability. If not, signals possible internal fracture.

trigger_points.condition: Iran's missile stockpile depleted to point of inability to sustain retaliation
trigger_points.mechanism: Missile launch rates already declining. If Iran can't sustain retaliation, Hormuz enforcement weakens.

trigger_points.condition: Iran accepts direct talks with US (publicly acknowledged)
trigger_points.mechanism: Would be biggest de-escalation signal. Currently receiving intermediary messages while publicly denying. Public acknowledgment would crash oil, rally equities.

trigger_points.condition: Iran retaliatory strike for Tangsiri assassination
trigger_points.mechanism: Historical pattern: Iran retaliated for Soleimani killing (2020 Ain al-Asad strike). IRGC Navy chief assassination at Bandar Abbas may trigger escalated naval action at Hormuz or strikes on Israeli/US assets. No retaliation confirmed yet as of March 28.
